# Plan — 2026-05-14

## Summary

Based on research from competitor analysis (Claude Code v2.1.141, Kilo, Cline CLI v3.0.2), this plan addresses 5 high-value gaps in Alexi. All items are self-contained, non-overlapping with the 9 existing open `auto-implement` issues, and ordered by impact-to-effort ratio.

## Issues Created

| # | Title | Size | Category |
|---|-------|------|----------|
| 1 | #314 [mcp] Pass ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR to MCP stdio server child processes | small | mcp |
| 2 | #315 [provider] Agent observability headers for subagent tracing | small | provider |
| 3 | #312 [config] Configurable auto-compaction threshold | small | config |
| 4 | #316 [config] Hook rejection feedback loop (continueOnBlock) | medium | config |
| 5 | #313 [command] Selective partial compaction (/compact-before) | medium | command |

## Implementation Details

### 1. [mcp] Pass ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR to MCP stdio server child processes

**Files to modify:**
- `src/mcp/client.ts` — Add `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR` to env in `connectStdio()` (line 133-136)
- `tests/mcp-config.test.ts` — Add test verifying the env var is set

**Approach:**
- In the `env` object construction at line 133, add `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR: process.cwd()` 
- The value should be `process.cwd()` as the MCP servers need the project root where Alexi was invoked
- Single line change, very high value for MCP servers that resolve relative paths

**Tests:**
- Unit test mocking `spawn` and asserting env includes `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR`

---

### 2. [provider] Agent observability headers for subagent tracing

**Files to modify:**
- `src/providers/sessionHeaders.ts` — Extend interface and builder functions to accept agent identity
- `tests/providers/sessionHeaders.test.ts` (new) — Test header construction

**Approach:**
- Add `agentID` and `parentAgentID` optional fields to `SessionContext` interface
- Add `x-alexi-agent-id` and `x-alexi-parent-agent-id` to built headers when present
- Update `buildSessionHeaders` and `mergeSessionHeaders` signatures
- This is backwards-compatible; existing callers without agent IDs get the same headers as before

**Tests:**
- Unit test that headers include agent identity when provided
- Unit test that headers omit agent fields when not provided (backwards compatibility)

---

### 3. [config] Configurable auto-compaction threshold

**Files to modify:**
- `src/compaction/index.ts` — Make `DEFAULT_WARNING_THRESHOLD` overridable via options
- `src/core/compaction.ts` — Allow `triggerThreshold` to be passed from config
- `src/core/sessionManager.ts` — Accept `compactionThreshold` in `SessionManagerOptions`
- `src/config/userConfig.ts` — Add typed accessor for `compactionThreshold`
- `src/core/__tests__/sessionManager.test.ts` — Test threshold propagation

**Approach:**
- Add `compactionThreshold?: number` (0.0-1.0) to `SessionManagerOptions`
- Pass it through to the compaction check in `sessionManager.ts:152`
- Allow setting via `~/.alexi/config.json` `{ "compactionThreshold": 0.7 }`
- Default remains 0.8 (80%) for backwards compatibility
- The `effortLevel.ts` already defines per-effort thresholds; this user config would serve as an override

**Tests:**
- Unit test: custom threshold triggers compaction at expected token count
- Unit test: default threshold unchanged when not configured

---

### 4. [config] Hook rejection feedback loop (continueOnBlock)

**Files to modify:**
- `src/hooks/index.ts` — Add `continueOnBlock` option to `HookDefinition`, modify `execute()` return type
- `src/core/orchestrator.ts` — When PreToolUse hook fails with `continueOnBlock: true`, inject error message back to model
- `tests/hooks/continueOnBlock.test.ts` (new) — Test rejection feedback flow

**Approach:**
- Add `continueOnBlock?: boolean` to `HookDefinition` interface and Zod schema
- When a `PreToolUse` hook returns `success: false` and `continueOnBlock` is true:
  - Instead of halting, return the `error` string from `HookResult` as a user message to the model
  - The orchestrator should treat this as a soft rejection: "Tool X was blocked: {reason}. Please adjust your approach."
- This requires a new return type from the hooks executor that distinguishes "halt" from "block-and-continue"
- Add `blocked?: boolean` and `blockReason?: string` to `HookResult`

**Tests:**
- Test that hook with `continueOnBlock: true` returns block info instead of halting
- Test that hook without `continueOnBlock` still halts (backwards compatibility)
- Integration test in orchestrator that blocked tool re-prompts the model

---

### 5. [command] Selective partial compaction (/compact-before)

**Files to modify:**
- `src/compaction/index.ts` — Add `upToIndex` parameter to `CompactionOptions`, implement partial compaction
- `src/core/compaction.ts` — Add `compactBefore(messages, index)` function
- `src/cli/interactive.ts` — Add `/compact-before <N>` command handler
- `src/cli/utils/completer.ts` — Register the new command in completions
- `src/core/__tests__/compaction.test.ts` — Test partial compaction logic

**Approach:**
- Add `upToIndex?: number` to `CompactionOptions` in `src/compaction/index.ts`
- When `upToIndex` is set, only compact messages `[0, upToIndex)` while preserving `[upToIndex, end]` verbatim
- The `/compact-before` command takes an integer argument (message index) and triggers partial compaction
- System prompt and recent messages after the index remain untouched
- Summarization applies only to the specified range

**Tests:**
- Unit test: partial compaction preserves messages after index
- Unit test: partial compaction summarizes messages before index
- Unit test: `/compact-before` with invalid index shows error message
